description: Fix invisible text in code blocks across the docs site. The CliCommand component, fenced code blocks, and inline code all have color/contrast issues where text is invisible or nearly invisible against dark backgrounds.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix invisible text in CliCommand component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the CLI install commands so that I can copy and use them
acceptanceCriteria: CliCommand text is visible in light mode (light text on dark background)
CliCommand text is visible in dark mode (light text on dark background)
TabPanels uses text-secondary-foreground instead of text-accent-foreground
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/components/ComponentInstall.tsx

The bug is on line 58:
```
<TabPanels className="text-accent-foreground text-sm">
```

`text-accent-foreground` resolves to `--accent-foreground: #000` in BOTH light and dark mode (see docs/app/global.css lines 49 and 69). The parent TabGroup on line 46 has `bg-secondary` which is `--secondary: #000` in light mode and `--secondary: #3a3a3a` in dark mode. This makes text completely invisible in light mode (black on black) and nearly invisible in dark mode (black on dark gray).

Fix: Change `text-accent-foreground` to `text-secondary-foreground` on line 58. The `--secondary-foreground` value is `#fff` in light mode and `#f5f5f5` in dark mode, which provides proper contrast against the `bg-secondary` background. This also matches the `text-secondary-foreground/90` already used on the parent TabGroup container on line 46.

userStories.title: Fix invisible text in fenced code blocks (CodeBlock component)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to read code examples in fenced code blocks so that I can follow along with documentation
acceptanceCriteria: Fenced code blocks have explicit light text color on their dark bg-code-bg background
Text is visible in both light and dark mode
A --code-fg CSS variable is added to the theme for code block foreground color
CodeBlock component uses the new text-code-fg class
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/components/CodeBlock.tsx and docs/app/global.css

The CodeBlock component (line 25) applies `bg-code-bg` but no text color:
```
<pre className={cn('bg-code-bg overflow-x-auto rounded-(--radius) p-4', className)} {...props}>
```

The `bg-code-bg` resolves to `--code-bg: #282a36` in light mode and `--code-bg: #1e1e2e` in dark mode — both are dark backgrounds. But text inherits `text-foreground` which is `#000` in light mode — black text on a dark background is invisible.

For code blocks WITH a language specifier (e.g. ```bash), rehype-pretty-code applies inline color styles to each token span, so they ARE visible. But code blocks WITHOUT a language specifier have no inline styles and fall back to the inherited color, making them invisible.

Fix approach:
1. In docs/app/global.css, add a `--code-fg` CSS variable to both `:root` and `.dark` blocks. Use `#f8f8f2` for both (the standard Dracula theme foreground color, matching the existing .hljs rule on line 100).
2. In the `@theme inline` block, add `--color-code-fg: var(--code-fg);` to register it with Tailwind.
3. In docs/components/CodeBlock.tsx line 25, add `text-code-fg` to the pre element's className.

This ensures all fenced code blocks have light text, whether or not rehype-pretty-code applies inline styles. The inline styles from rehype-pretty-code will override the base color for syntax-highlighted tokens.

userStories.title: Remove dead hljs CSS rules from global.css
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the CSS to contain only rules that are actually applied so that the codebase stays clean and maintainable
acceptanceCriteria: All .hljs CSS rules (lines 86-161 of global.css) are removed
The comment 'Syntax Highlighting stuff' section header is removed
No visual regression — the site uses rehype-pretty-code/Shiki which applies inline styles, not hljs classes
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/app/global.css

Lines 86-161 contain `.hljs` CSS rules from a Dracula highlight.js theme. However, the docs site uses `rehype-pretty-code` (configured in docs/content-collections.ts with the `dracula-soft` Shiki theme), which applies inline styles to `<span>` elements — it does NOT generate `hljs-*` classes. These CSS rules are dead code that never match any elements.

Remove everything from line 86 (the `/* Syntax Highlighting stuff */` comment) through line 161 (the closing brace of `.hljs-emphasis`). Keep the `/* Custom scrollbar for sidebar */` section and everything after it.

Note: US-002 already added the `--code-fg` variable which replaces the only useful value from these rules (the `#f8f8f2` foreground color from `.hljs` on line 100).
